shots.title: Guard framework + hook generation
shots.notes: New guard.ts in core with types, 6 guard definitions, formatters, and Claude Code settings.json generator. New slope guard CLI command. slope hook add --level=full installs all guards. Added guidance config to SlopeConfig. 18 new core tests.

shots.title: PreToolUse guards — explore + hazard
shots.notes: Explore guard checks for codebase index files and suggests checking them. Hazard guard looks up common issues by file area and injects warnings. Both non-blocking (additionalContext only). 10 new CLI tests.

shots.title: Discipline hooks — commit nudge + scope drift
shots.notes: Commit nudge checks git log for time since last commit/push with configurable thresholds. Scope drift checks if edited file is outside claimed areas. Both non-blocking. 7 new CLI tests.

shots.title: Session hooks — compaction checkpoint + stop check
shots.notes: Compaction guard records event to store on PreCompact. Stop check blocks session end when uncommitted/unpushed work exists. 6 new CLI tests.

conditions.description: Context continued from previous session — required reconstruction from summary
Research spike on Claude Code hooks API informed architecture

nutrition.description: Full build+test+typecheck after every ticket (675 tests at completion)
Commit-per-ticket discipline — 4 commits for 4 tickets, pushed after each
41 new tests: 18 core (guard framework) + 23 CLI (guards + init)
Zero hazards — cleanest sprint yet. Research spike before implementation prevented common issue #8 (TS strict config cast).

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Third consecutive par sprint. The guard framework fell into place naturally — the research on Claude Code's hook API upfront was key. All 6 guards implemented with clean separation between core types and CLI handlers.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: Guards use additionalContext for non-blocking guidance — never deny/block for guidance hooks. Only stop-check uses blockReason because it's the Stop event. The guard dispatcher script at .claude/hooks/slope-guard.sh routes all hooks to `slope guard <name>`.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: Claude Code's hook system is more capable than expected — PreToolUse can inject context, modify tool input, and control permissions. The matcher regex system is clean. The research spike paid for itself immediately.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: The guidance layer is complete. S13 brings HTML reports and launch polish — the last sprint before multi-agent orchestration.

yardage_book_updates: packages/core/src/guard.ts: HookInput, GuardResult, PreToolUseOutput, PostToolUseOutput, StopOutput, GUARD_DEFINITIONS
slope guard <name>: CLI command reads hook JSON from stdin, runs handler, outputs response JSON
slope hook add --level=full: installs all 6 guards into .claude/settings.json
Guards: explore (PreToolUse Read|Glob|Grep), hazard (PreToolUse Edit|Write), commit-nudge (PostToolUse Edit|Write), scope-drift (PreToolUse Edit|Write), compaction (PreCompact), stop-check (Stop)
SlopeConfig.guidance: disabled, indexPaths, hazardRecency, commitInterval, pushInterval, scopeDrift
.claude/hooks/slope-guard.sh: dispatcher script that routes to slope guard handlers
course_management_notes: 4 tickets, 4 in_the_hole — zero misses, zero hazards (3rd consecutive clean sprint)
Tests grew from 634 to 675 (+41 across core and cli)
7 new files: guard.ts, guard CLI, 4 guard handlers, guard tests
Research spike on Claude Code hooks API before implementation — prevented architectural missteps
